content.full_text:
June 16, 1975


Mr. Frank R. Manzano
Senior Assistant City Attorney
613 East Broadway
Glendale, California 91205

Dear Mr. Manzano:

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-128

Thank you for your letter of May 30, 1975 requesting an
opinion with respect to certain conflicts between the Governmental Conflict of Interests Act and the Political Reform Act.

This letter is to advise you that your request for a formal
opinion is denied. It is not within the jurisdiction of the
Fair Political Practices Commission to issue legal opinions
regarding the Governmental Conflict of Interests Act. If there
is a conflict between the Political Reform Act and the Governmental Conflict of Interests Act, the Political Reform Act
would prevail by reason of Government Code Section 81013 and
by reason of the general doctrine that a later statute prevails
over an earlier statute. There appears to be divided opinion
as to whether a conflict in fact exists, but most attorneys I
have spoken with have agreed that the prudent course of action
is to assume that both acts are in effect.

It may be helpful to bring to your attention AB 494,
authored by Assemblyman Berman, which would clarify the present
status of the Governmental Conflict of Interests Act. A copy
of the bill is enclosed.

Although for the reason stated no formal opinion will be
issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently helpful. If you
have any further questions, I urge you to contact a member of
the Commission staff.

Sincerely,


Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the
Commission

DHL:cjb
Enclosure

Request No. 75 125
Received________
Response Due________

City of GLENDALE                                    CALIFORNIA

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
                                                   613 EAST BROADWAY
                                                   GLENDALE, CALIFORNIA 91205
                                                   TELEPHONE (213) 956-2030

                                    May 30, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

                    Re:    Political Reform Act of 1974
                           (Proposition 9).

Gentlemen:

        Since the approval by the voters in June, 1974,
of the Political Reform Act of 1974 (Proposition 9) there
appears to be a conflict between the provisions of certain
sections of the said Act with the Governmental Conflict of
Interests Act (Section 3600-3760 of the Government Code).
The particular area in the Political Reform Act of 1974
which we feel is in conflict with the Governmental Conflict
of Interests Act is Article 1, Chapter 7, commencing with
Sections 87100 through 87312, and Chapter 11 commencing with
Sections 91000 through 91014.

        The above mentioned sections appear to, in some
areas, duplicate the wording of the Governmental Conflict of
Interests Act and, also, to provide for a different method of
enforcement of any violation by an officer of a municipality
in doing a prohibited act which is in essence prohibited by
the two Acts.

        In view of this conflict we would appreciate your
opinion on the following issues:

        1.   Did Proposition 9 impliedly repeal the
             Governmental Conflict of Interests Act
             (Government Code Sections 3600-3750)?

        2.   If Proposition 9 did not impliedly repeal
             the Governmental Conflict of Interests Act
             but where there is a direct conflict between
             the provisions of Proposition 9 and the

provisions of the Governmental Conflict
of Interests Act, which provision would
prevail?

Thank you for your consideration and assistance in
this matter.

Very truly yours,

RICHARD W. MARSTON, CITY ATTORNEY

By: [signature]
FRANK K. MANZANO
Senior Assistant City Attorney

FRM:kj

-2-
